answer:
The woman has a single-gene deletion (α-thalassemia trait), and the man has a two-gene deletion in trans (α-thalassemia minor). Their offspring can inherit either the woman’s normal or deleted allele and either the man’s normal or deleted allele, but since the man’s deletions are on different chromosomes, each child will always receive one deleted gene from him. Therefore, 50% of offspring will inherit the woman’s deleted gene as well, resulting in a two-gene deletion.

\boxed{B}
